name: Amit Kapoor
title: Director, Product Management & Partner Strategy
aboutBio: Executive professional with 20+ years of experience in product management and partner relations. I help organizations meet technical and business objectives by defining technology, partner and customer strategies. I have led organizations in product management and partner relations to gain market share, grow revenues and innovate technology. At Verizon, I focus on how advanced networking — SD-WAN, virtual network services, and 5G — can be turned into real enterprise productivity gains. I am a firm believer that the outcome is what customers will consume, and that technology only matters when it delivers measurable results.

recommendations.text: Amit is a rare breed of engineer that bridges both his technologist and business acumen characteristics. Amit is extremely technical and will roll his sleeves up and get his hands dirty, all the while building relationships with his customers and closing the deal.
Amit is a very conscientious manager who is astute with both a broad range of technical knowledge and expertise coupled with years of commercial acumen.

visionQuotes: Productivity is the key outcome that our businesses are looking for — not technology for its own sake.
Sometimes an outcome is a measure of what an application does in the network and how you are enabling that need. At the end of the day, the outcome is what customers will consume.
Building closed loop assurance — a self-healing network — is what allows enterprises to move from reactive IT to proactive business enablement.
strategies: Position Verizon's virtual network services as productivity tools, not infrastructure products
Build partner ecosystems that allow enterprises to adopt SD-WAN and 5G without operational disruption
Leverage AI and machine intelligence to create self-healing, automated network environments for enterprise customers
Drive product strategy that connects network capability to measurable business outcomes
Develop interoperability frameworks that allow Verizon to co-go-to-market effectively with technology partners
challenges: Competing with hyperscaler-native networking propositions from AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud
Translating complex network technology — SD-WAN, MEC, private 5G — into clear business value narratives
Managing a large, multi-vendor partner ecosystem with varying capability and commitment levels
Keeping pace with the rapid evolution of enterprise networking requirements driven by cloud and AI adoption
Differentiating Verizon's product portfolio in a market where connectivity is increasingly commoditised
opportunities: Scale private 5G and mobile edge computing propositions for enterprise manufacturing and logistics clients
Expand SD-WAN market share by embedding AI-driven network assurance as a standard feature
Build deeper integration between Verizon network products and the major cloud platforms
Develop outcome-based commercial models for network services tied to customer productivity metrics
Grow healthcare and public sector vertical network programmes leveraging Verizon's security and compliance credentials
trendsInsights: Enterprise SD-WAN adoption is accelerating as organisations move multi-cloud workloads and require intelligent traffic management
Private 5G is moving from pilot to production in manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare environments
AI-driven network management is reducing mean time to resolution and enabling proactive rather than reactive operations
The boundary between network and cloud is dissolving — enterprises expect seamless, software-defined connectivity across both
Partner ecosystems are becoming critical differentiators as no single vendor can deliver the full enterprise technology stack
competitorsPartners: AT&T Business, T-Mobile for Business (primary US telecom competitors)
Ericsson (key technology partner — 5G and closed loop assurance)
AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud (cloud ecosystem partners)
productsTechnologies: Verizon SD-WAN (Cisco IWAN, Viptela, Versa, Silver Peak)
targetMarkets: Large enterprise and Fortune 500 IT organisations
Healthcare systems requiring secure, compliant networking
Manufacturing and logistics with private 5G and IoT requirements
Technology and media companies with high-bandwidth network demands
kpiMetrics: Verizon Business Group annual revenue approximately $30 billion
Verizon ranked #1 network quality for 35 consecutive reporting periods
SD-WAN portfolio expanded through Silver Peak and Versa additions
Multiple patents held in network design, configuration, and virtual network services
Verizon Business serves over 99% of Fortune 500 companies
decisionInsights: Outcome-first framing — evaluates technology partnerships based on measurable enterprise productivity impact
Technical depth combined with commercial awareness — equally comfortable in engineering and business conversations
Partner-oriented — values co-development and ecosystem collaboration over zero-sum vendor competition
Data-driven decision making — uses network performance and customer outcome metrics to prioritise product investments
Long-term relationship builder — career history of sustained partnerships across multiple technology cycles
risksThreats: Hyperscaler networking products increasingly competing with telco SD-WAN and virtual network services
Price commoditisation in enterprise connectivity making value differentiation harder
Partner ecosystem complexity increasing coordination overhead and slowing go-to-market velocity
AI-native networking startups disrupting established telco product paradigms
Enterprise buyer preference shifting toward platform consolidation with fewer, larger vendors
quotes: At Verizon, one of the key things we are trying to do is build what's called closed loop assurance — a self-healing network that allows an application to have a brownout and then have the entire orchestration engine self-heal based on assurance metrics.
Productivity is the key outcome that our businesses are looking for.
bio: Amit Kapoor is a Director of Product Management and Partner Strategy at Verizon Business with over 20 years of experience in network technology, product management, and commercial leadership. He has led Verizon's Virtual Network Services practice including SD-WAN product strategy and partner development, and has represented Verizon in industry forums on topics including 5G enterprise, closed loop assurance, and AI-driven network management. He holds multiple patents in network design and virtual network services.
awards: Multiple Verizon patents in network design and virtual network configuration
ONUG (Open Networking User Group) speaker and contributor
